engineering.translation: Where the cittam (mind) comes structurally to rest — niruddham, restrained — through yoga-sevayā (the practice of yoga); where, perceiving the Self by the self (ātmanā ātmānaṁ paśyan), one is satisfied within the Self (ātmani tuṣyati). The verse opens the four-verse samādhi-block (6.20-23). Both clauses must hold simultaneously: the mind's restraint, and the satisfaction-in-Self that follows. Shankara identifies this state with samādhi proper — the mind, restrained from wandering, is withdrawn through yoga-practice; perceiving the Self by the mind purified through concentration. Ramanuja: the mind subdued everywhere by repeated practice rejoices, indifferent to all other objects. The verse describes a state of consciousness whose metaphysical reach exceeds operational engineering. STRETCHED honestly: the engineering analog scopes downward to the mind that has organised itself such that the work-state is supplied internally rather than externally.
engineering.concrete_scenario: A staff engineer at a mid-stage observability startup sits at her desk on a Tuesday morning, working through a Datadog dashboard configuration for the Prometheus exporter migration. There is no upcoming review. There is no deadline. There is no manager waiting on output. The work itself supplies the state: the puzzle of figuring out how to deduplicate metric labels without breaking historical queries holds her attention because the puzzle is interesting, not because circumstances demand it. Three hours pass. She has not checked Slack; she has not refreshed the GitHub PR queue; she has not glanced at the metrics on her own service. The work is its own reward at this narrow operational scope. Compare the engineer who at the same desk requires constant external prods — the imminent demo, the PR review approaching, the manager's check-in, the metric watching her — to maintain engagement; without those wind-supplies, the focus collapses within twenty minutes. The first engineer's mind is operationally ātmani tuṣyati, satisfied within (the work) itself. The verse reaches further than engineering can travel — its full claim is samādhi, consciousness rejoicing in non-dual ātman, not engineer-rejoicing-in-puzzle. The scope-mismatch is honest and tagged.
engineering.falsifiability: The analog fails if a reader reads the engineering rung as equivalent to samādhi. The verse names a metaphysical state whose reach exceeds any operational engineering analog — the mind perceiving the Self by the Self, satisfied within the Self. Engineer-absorbed-in-work is not that. An engineer who claims to have attained 6.20 by enjoying their work has flattened the verse into a productivity-coaching reading and missed the metaphysics entirely.
engineering.counter_example: When the apparent absorption is in fact escape — the engineer who hides inside the comfortable refactor because the production incident is unbearable, or who buries themselves in the technical puzzle to avoid the difficult conversation with the team — the verse does not endorse this. Niruddham cittam is a positive restraint resulting from yoga-sevayā, not from avoidance. The mind has been stilled by practice, not by withdrawal-from-life.
engineering.implication: Audit which inputs to your engineering state are currently external supplies (the deadline, the manager's interest, the upcoming review) versus internal supplies (the work itself, the puzzle's structure). Where the supply is external, the operational rung the verse names is not yet present.
engineering.quotable_line: When the work itself supplies the state, the wind has been removed at the source.

iterations.mutation: v0 generation: BG 6.20 opens samādhi-block (6.20-23) per chapter-6 thesis. STRETCHED honestly because the verse's metaphysical reach (perceiving Self-by-self; samādhi) exceeds operational engineering. Engineering analog scoped downward to internal-supply-of-work-state. Both clauses (niruddham + ātmani tuṣyati) preserved. Shankara/Ramanuja preserved. Tools: Datadog, Prometheus, GitHub. Confidence MEDIUM.
